label: Glass Modern
description: Brand-forward authentication surface with subtle glassmorphism, gradient backgrounds, and modern product polish — for SaaS that wants the auth flow to feel like the rest of the app.
signatureMotif: soft glass card hovering on a subtle brand gradient with crisp typography and gentle motion
promptHints: Make the auth surface feel brand-forward — like a beautifully crafted product moment, not a utility form.
Use a subtle gradient or aurora background and a single elevated glass-style card that anchors the screen.

layouts: Use a centered glass card (440-500px wide) floating on a soft brand gradient background, with the brand mark above the form and small auxiliary links below.
Use a split layout where the left side carries a calm brand statement, product screenshot, or testimonial and the right side holds a clean auth form on a slightly translucent panel.
Use a single-column onboarding flow with a sticky brand bar on top, a glass card in the middle, and progress dots near the footer rather than a heavy stepper.
Use a magic-link or email-verification confirmation page with the same glass card frame, a large success-state icon, and a calm 'open your inbox'-style instruction.
Use a recovery or 2FA page where the field is centered inside the glass card with reassuring helper text, leaving the gradient backdrop visible around the edges.
motifs: Soft glass cards with backdrop-blur, semi-transparent backgrounds, 1rem-1.25rem radii, and gentle 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) shadows.
Subtle multi-stop brand gradients (or aurora) on the page background, kept low-contrast so form text reads cleanly even on light themes.
Generous spacing inside the card with clear primary/secondary action hierarchy and small social-login buttons aligned in a single row.
Quiet motion: the card fades + lifts in on mount, inputs animate their focus ring smoothly, success states cross-fade rather than swap abruptly.
antiPatterns: Avoid the dense product-form look of clean-auth — this variant should feel airy and consumer-grade, not like an internal admin tool.
Never use heavy hard borders, brutalist edges, or stacked corporate forms; the visual signature relies on softness.
Avoid noisy decorative blobs, oversized illustrations, or marketing copy crammed into the form area — the gradient should do the visual work.
Do not pile on shadows, gradients, AND borders at the same time; pick one elevation cue per surface so the glass effect stays believable.
